title: nf-core/pixelator pipeline parameters
description: Pipeline for analysis of Proximity Network Assay data

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/pixelator/usage#samplesheet-input).

input_basedir.description: Path to a local or remote directory that is the "current working directory" for relative paths defined in the input samplesheet

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

save_pna_amplicon_reads.description: Save intermediate amplicon reads generated from the raw input reads.
save_pna_amplicon_reads.help: By default, generated amplicon FastQ files will not be saved to the results directory. Specify this flag (or set it to `true` in your config file) to copy these files to the results directory when complete.

pna_amplicon_mismatches.description: The number of mismatches allowed while anchoring reads using LBS sequences (in percentage of seen LBS length) [default: 0.1; 0.0<=x<=0.5]

pna_amplicon_remove_polyg.description: Remove PolyG sequences (length of 10 or more)

pna_amplicon_quality_cutoff.description: Minimum average quality a read must have. Pass 0 to disable quality filtering.

pna_amplicon_low_complexity_filter.description: Enable filtering of amplicons with low complexity UMI sequences.

pna_amplicon_low_complexity_threshold.description: The threshold for determining if a UMI sequence is of low complexity and will be removed. [0.5<=x<=1.0]

pna_amplicon_lbs_filter.description: Enable filtering of amplicons with UMI sequences that show similarity to the LBS sequence.

pna_amplicon_lbs_filter_min_overlap.description: The minimum overlap required in a semi-global alignment of a UMI sequence with the LBS regions. Reads with less overlap will not be removed by the LBS filter. [4<=x<=28]

pna_amplicon_lbs_filter_error_rate.description: The allowed error-rate in a semi-global alignment of a UMI sequence with the LBS regions. The error rate is defined as a percentage of UMI length. [0.0<=x<=1.0]

pna_demux_mismatches.description: The number of mismatches allowed in marker barcodes.

pna_demux_output_chunk_reads.description: The target number of reads in a single file of the partitioned demux output.
pna_demux_output_chunk_reads.help: This option can be used to fine-tune the parallelization of the subsequent collapse stage. A smaller value will create split the demux molecule list into more and smaller parts.

pna_demux_output_max_chunks.description: The maximum number of chunks that the demuxed ouput for each umi region will be split into
pna_demux_output_max_chunks.help: This option is used to control the parallelization of the subsequent collapse stage.

pna_demux_strategy.description: The demux and collapsing strategy to use
pna_demux_strategy.help:
**paired:** Use the paired collapsing strategy. This is the default and recommended strategy for PNA data.

**independent:** Use the independent collapsing strategy. This strategy will corrects errors in UMI regions independently. It provides performance benefits but is experimental.

save_pna_demux_parquet.description: Save intermediate parquet files containing embeddings of all reads that contain valid antibody barcodes.
save_pna_demux_parquet.help: By default, parquet files containing embeddings of reads with valid antibody barcodes will not be saved to the results directory. Specify this flag (or set to `true` in your config file) to copy these files to the results directory when complete.

save_pna_demux_passed_reads.description: Save intermediate FASTQC read files containing all reads that contain valid antibody barcodes.
save_pna_demux_passed_reads.help: By default, FastQ files containing reads with valid antibody barcodes will not be saved to the results directory. Specify this flag (or set to `true` in your config file) to copy these files to the results directory when complete.

save_pna_demux_failed_reads.description: Save intermediate FASTQC read files containing all reads that do not contain valid antibody barcodes.
save_pna_demux_failed_reads.help: By default, FastQ files containing reads without valid antibody barcodes will not be saved to the results directory. Specify this flag (or set to `true` in your config file) to copy these files to the results directory when complete.

pna_collapse_algorithm.description: The algorithm to use for collapsing.
pna_collapse_algorithm.help:
**cluster:** Form networks of connected UMIs (based on hamming distance threshold). Each connected component is a read group. In the above example, all the UMIs are contained in a single connected component and thus there is one read group containing all reads, with ACGT as the `selected` UMI.

**directional (default):** Form networks with edges defined based on hamming distance threshold and node A counts >= (2 * node B counts) - 1. Each connected component is a read group, with the node with the highest counts selected as the top node for the component.

pna_collapse_mismatches.description: The number of mismatches allowed when collapsing (adjacency)

save_pna_collapsed_reads.description: Save an intermediate parquet file containing collapsed read information.
save_pna_collapsed_reads.help: By default, intermediate collapsed reads will not be saved to the results directory. Specify this flag (or set to `true` in your config file) to copy these files to the results directory when complete.

pna_graph_options.title: Options for pixelator graph command on PNA data.

save_pna_graph_pixelfile.description: Save the PXL dataset after the graph stage.
save_pna_graph_pixelfile.help: By default, the PXL file after graph will not be saved to the results directory unless `--skip_analysis` and `--skip_layout` is passed. Specify this flag (or set to `true` in your config file) to copy these files to the results directory when complete.

pna_graph_multiplet_recovery.description: Activate the multiplet recovery using leiden community detection

pna_graph_leiden_iterations.description: Number of iterations for the leiden algorithm.
pna_graph_leiden_iterations.help: High values will decrease the variance of the results but increase the runtime

pna_graph_initial_stage_leiden_resolutio.description: The resolution parameter for the leiden algorithm at the initial stage.
pna_graph_initial_stage_leiden_resolutio.help: This should typically be set higher than the refinement stage resolution.

pna_graph_refinement_stage_leiden_resolu.description: The resolution parameter for the leiden algorithm at the refinement stage.
pna_graph_refinement_stage_leiden_resolu.help: This should typically be set lower than the initial stage resolution.

pna_graph_min_count.description: Discard edges with a read count below given value. Set to 1 to disable filtering.

pna_graph_min_component_size_in_refineme.description: The minimum component size to consider for refinement

pna_graph_max_refinement_recursion_depth.description: The maximum recursion depth for the refinement algorithm. Set to 1 to disable refinement.

pna_graph_initial_stage_max_edges_to_rem.description: The maximum number of edges to remove between components during the initial stage (iteration == 0) of multiplet recovery.

pna_graph_refinement_stage_max_edges_to_.description: The maximum number of edges to remove between components during the refinement stage (iteration > 0) of multiplet recovery.

pna_graph_initial_stage_max_edges_to_rem.description: The maximum number of edges to remove between two components relative to the number of nodes in the smaller of the two when during the initial stage (iteration == 0) of multiplet recovery.

pna_graph_refinement_stage_max_edges_to_.description: The maximum number of edges to remove between two components relative to the number of nodes in the smaller of the two when during the refinement stage (iteration > 0) of multiplet recovery.

pna_graph_graph_min_component_size_to_pr.description: The minimum number of nodes in an potential new components in order for it to be pruned.

pna_graph_component_size_min_threshold.description: Components with fewer nodes than this will be filtered from the output data. Set to null to enable automatic size filtering based on the data.

save_pna_denoise_pixelfile.description: Save the PXL dataset after the denoise stage.
save_pna_denoise_pixelfile.help: By default, the PXL file after denoise will not be saved to the results directory.

pna_denoise_run_one_core_graph_denoising.description: Activate the one-core graph denoising algorithm

pna_denoise_pval_threshold.description: The p-value threshold for the a marker to be considered significantly over-expressed in the one-core layer of a component

pna_denoise_inflate_factor.description: The inflate factor for the number of nodes from over-expressed markers to be removed from the one-core layer of a component

save_pna_analysis_pixelfile.description: Save the PXL dataset after the analysis stage.
save_pna_analysis_pixelfile.help: By default, the PXL dataset after the analysis stage will only be saved be saved when `--skip_layout` is passed. Specify this flag (or set to `true` in your config file) to copy these files to the results directory when complete.

pna_analysis_compute_k_cores.description: Compute k-core summary tables for each component

pna_analysis_proximity_nbr_of_permutatio.description: Number of permutations to use when computing the expected proximity scores

pna_analysis_compute_svd_var_explained.description: Compute the variance explained by the SVD components

pna_analysis_svd_nbr_of_pivots.description: Number of pivots to use for the SVD decomposition

pna_layout_options.title: Options for pixelator single-cell-pna layout command.

pna_layout_no_node_marker_counts.description: Skip adding marker counts to the layout.

pna_layout_layout_algorithm.description: Select a layout algorithm to use. This can be specified as a comma separated list to compute multiple layouts. Possible values are: pmds, pmds_3d, wpmds, wpmds_3d.

pna_layout_pmds_pivots.description: Number of pivots to use for the PMDS layout algorithm. Default: 50. More gives better results, but increases computation time.

pna_layout_wpmds_k.description: The window size used when computing probability weights for the wpmds layout method. Only used when layout algorithm is set to wpmds.

global_config_options.title: Global options
global_config_options.description: Global configuration options specific to nf-core/pixelator.

pixelator_container.description: Override the container image reference to use for all steps using the `pixelator` command.
pixelator_container.help_text:
Use this to force the pipeline to use a different image version in all steps that use the pixelator command.
The pipeline is not guaranteed to work when using different pixelator versions.

experiment_summary_container.description: Override the container image to use for the experiment summary step.
experiment_summary_container.help_text:
Use this to force the pipeline to use a different image version for the experiment summary step.
The pipeline is not guaranteed to work when using different experiment summary versions.

save_all.help: This option is equivalent of passing all: `--save_* and --save_pna_*` options.

save_json.description: Save all JSON report and metadata files generated by pixelator.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.

help_full.description: Display the full detailed help message.

show_hidden.description: Display hidden parameters in the help message (only works when --help or --help_full are provided).
